title: androcalva gaudichaudii
intro: Androcalva gaudichaudii, also known as the Green Finger or Finger Lime Bush, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia. It is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae, characterized by its finger-like green fruits and small white flowers. The medicinal properties of Androcalva gaudichaudii include its high concentration of citric acid and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in weight loss, lower cholesterol levels, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, and it can be prepared as an herbal tea or used in topical applications.
